producer_classification_evidence._budget_or_policy_fallback: Reached on high-complexity public/internal fallback; final output scrubbed by shared invariant helper when flag is not exactly True.
producer_classification_evidence._external_candidate_proposal: _qualifies_for_external_candidate requires external_routing_enabled is True, so the producer is not reached when the flag is not exactly True.
producer_classification_evidence._private_provider_boundary: Reached on raw-private provider-boundary input regardless of flag; final output scrubbed by shared invariant helper when flag is not exactly True.
producer_classification_evidence._unknown_external_pressure: Current rule-8 branch calls _external_disabled_local_fallback instead of the producer when external_routing_enabled is not exactly True.

_budget_or_policy_fallback.representative_trigger_input: public/internal high-complexity scientific input with no candidate tier available

_external_candidate_proposal.representative_trigger_input: public/internal high-complexity scientific-depth input with allowed provider/budget tiers

_unknown_external_pressure.representative_trigger_input: unknown sensitivity plus external pressure via needs_current_info

redaction_field_interpretation: Redaction fields are broader than external proposals because _block_secret uses them for local blocked secret handling; general invariant assertions therefore focus on proposal/provider/network/confirmation artifacts, with a dedicated provider-boundary downgrade test asserting redaction clearing.
branch_activation_limitations: Flag-off outputs normalize to the same local/no-external shape, so producer-specific branch activation is proven primarily by monkeypatch call counts rather than final route markers alone.
pre_commit_explicit_path_no_wiring_check: No production call to evaluate_external_budget_gate, no provider client code, and no external execution wiring were added in the E4-PRE files.
known_limitations: AST is used for producer discovery, not full dataflow proof.
E4-PRE protection is only as complete as producer discovery.
A future producer using a mutation or construction pattern not detected by AST or repo discovery may evade both the expected producer set and monkeypatch enforcement.
Behavioral matrix is example-based and may not exercise such a producer.
Monkeypatch enforcement depends on correct producer classification and representative trigger inputs.
A producer with incorrect or incomplete representative trigger input may appear unreachable even if it is reachable in production.
Positive preserve tests must use a valid proposed_external_target; otherwise downstream target validation can create false failures unrelated to over-scrubbing.
Expected producer set is a tripwire, not the main guarantee.
E4 still requires cumulative egress scope, digest and confirmation, provider policy, and E3 re-validation.
